Details:
Removal of perchlorate by electrochemical reduction can be effective (up to 99 percent removal) and is dependent on a number of factors, including: chemical treatment technology, temperature, pressure, contact time, iron type and dose, initial concentration, and pH. Removals above 90 percent were associated with temperatures higher than those encountered in drinking water treatment. Otherwise, removals were generally below 50 percent.
For the electrochemical reduction of perchlorate, removal was limited by competition of anions for the active sites on the electrode. Perchlorate was adsorbed less strongly in comparison to sulfate and chloride. Chemical treatment of perchlorate is currently being tested in bench-scale or batch studies. It is unknown if the percent removal achieved in the laboratory setting can be scaled to full-scale applications.
